feed.title: SAP Community - Certification
feed.subtitle: Certification blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Release of System-Based Certification for SAP Service Cloud Version 2
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

On behalf of the product learning team, we are pleased to announce the updated and first of its kind system based certification for SAP Service Cloud Version 2. 

SAP Certified - Implementation Consultant - SAP Service Cloud Version 2

This milestone marks a major shift in how SAP validates real-world skills by moving away from traditional theory-based assessments and toward practical, scenario-driven, hands-on evaluations.

 

🎯Who Is This Certification For?

This certification is designed for:
  • Implementation consultants
  • System administrators

🌟A New Era of Practical, System-Based Assessment

Unlike standard multiple-choice exams, this new certification provides candidates with:
  • A live SAP Service Cloud Version 2 system, and
  • A PDF containing a set of tasks to complete
Candidates perform actual configuration and scenario-based exercises directly in the system. Once all required tasks are completed, they can submit their results for automated evaluation. Within minutes, candidates will know whether they have passed or failed — offering a faster, more authentic, and more meaningful way to validate capability.
This practical approach ensures that certified consultants are not only knowledgeable but also skilled in applying SAP Service Cloud Version 2 functionality in realistic customer scenarios.
 

🎯Why System Based Certifications?

The reimagined SAP Certification exams offer a range of benefits. More specifically:

  • Job-ready proof: Demonstrates you can navigate SAP Service Cloud Version 2 and execute business-critical tasks—not just recall concepts.
  • Strong SAP signal: An SAP-recognised credential and digital badge that hiring managers and project leads across the ecosystem trust.
  • Practice over memorisation: Preparation aligns to SAP Learning Journeys and hands-on exercises for better retention and impact.
  • Always current: Content and recertification align with SAP’s cloud release cycle, reflecting the latest features and UI. 
  • Higher integrity than Q&A-only: Performance on practical tasks reduces guesswork and emphasises authentic ability.

📚How to Prepare

To set yourself up for success, we recommend the following preparation path:

1. Complete the SAP Service Cloud Version 2 Learning Courses

The new learning journey is available on learning.sap.com provide the essential and implementation knowledge you need before attempting the hands‑on tasks. 

2. Practice Using the Hands-On System Exercises

Strengthen your confidence by trying out interactive exercises on a practical system . This helps you become familiar with the interfaces, workflows, and configuration steps you’ll be expected to perform during the certification. 

 

📢Spread the Word!

Help us share this exciting update with partners, colleagues, and other stakeholders.

📝We Value Your Feedback

After completing the certification, please take a moment to share your feedback. Your insights help us continuously improve the learning experience.

entry.title: AGP’s Next Chapter: Dual SAP Certification Fuels Cloud Growth Ambitions
entry.content:
AGP, a specialized SAP service provider based in Chile, has reached an important milestone in its growth journey: achieving its first-ever certifications for SAP SuccessFactors HCM operations and SAP BTP operations. But for AGP, this achievement goes beyond formal recognition. It reflects a deliberate effort to strengthen quality, standardize processes, and further mature as an organization. In this interview, AGP shares what motivated them to pursue certification at this stage, how the audit process catalyzed internal improvement, and why they see certification as a foundation for long-term excellence rather than a one-time validation. The conversation also explores AGP’s expanding role in cloud transformation - building capabilities in integration, extensibility, and automation - and outlines its vision to become a leading SAP partner in the region.

Could you start by telling us about your company – what services do you offer, and which industries or customer types do you primarily serve?

AGP is a specialized SAP service provider focused on implementing, supporting, and optimizing solutions such as SAP SuccessFactors and SAP BTP. We primarily work with large-scale organizations across industries such as healthcare, mining, transportation, and services, where operational quality and business continuity are critical. Our main differentiator is our team: highly committed professionals with international experience who work closely with our clients, understand their businesses, and act as strategic partners.

AGP team

 

You were recently certified for the first time for SAP SuccessFactors HCM operations and SAP BTP operations. What motivated you to pursue these certifications at this point in your journey?

The certification represents a natural step in our evolution. We have been strengthening our SAP operations with a strong focus on quality, processes, and specialization, and these certifications formally validate that path. Our main motivation was to reassure our clients that we operate under SAP-recognized standards, reinforcing our value proposition based on trust, excellence, and delivery capability.

 

Looking back, how did you experience the audit process? And did it give you any new perspectives – whether on your internal governance, quality standards, or ways of working?

The process was very enriching. It allowed us to confirm that our practices are aligned with international standards while also identifying opportunities for improvement in governance, documentation, and standardization. More than an audit, we experienced it as a step forward in organizational maturity, reinforcing our commitment to continuous improvement.

 

SAP is strongly focused on the cloud. How are you already supporting customers in their migration, and where do you plan to expand?

We are already supporting our customers in their transition to the cloud, especially SAP SuccessFactors and SAP BTP solutions. We participate in both implementation and AMS services, ensuring the solutions' continuity, evolution, and adoption. Our expansion focus is on strengthening capabilities in integration, extensibility, and automation on SAP BTP, as well as enabling more agile and scalable cloud operating models.

How do you plan to differentiate yourself and leverage the certification – both in the market and in your communication with customers?

Our differentiation goes beyond the certification itself - it lies in how we work. The certification supports a model based on close client relationships, high-level teams, and a strong service culture. We will leverage it to reinforce market trust, demonstrating that we combine SAP standards with a human, flexible, and results-oriented approach.

Where do you see the biggest growth opportunities, and how do you plan to scale your team to meet demand?

We see significant opportunities in cloud adoption, especially in talent management solutions and innovation platforms like SAP BTP. To scale, we are strongly investing in local talent development, training and certifying new consultants, while strengthening our team with senior profiles who bring experience and culture. We believe sustainable growth is achieved by building strong teams, not just increasing capacity.

What are your strategic plans to deepen your partnership with SAP in the coming years?

Our goal is to position ourselves as a leading partner in the region. We aim to continue expanding our certifications, strengthening our cloud capabilities, and co-innovating with SAP to deliver real value to our customers. Our relationship with SAP is strategic, and we see it as a long-term partnership built on trust, quality, and shared growth.

 

 

Information on the certification program for operations partners, as well as an overview of all certified partners, can be found in the SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Visit OPC at SAP.com or on the SAP Partner Portal.

entry.title: Your SAP Sapphire Experience Starts Here - Explore the Learning Zone
entry.content:
Excited for SAP Sapphire and ready to elevate your learning journey? The learning zone for SAP Sapphire is now available for you to explore tailored resources, sharpen your expertise, prepare for certification exams, and connect with SAP experts. Get ready for the event and benefit from a 25% discount on SAP Certification as well as complementary access to the free SAP Learning Hub, event edition, including expert-led live sessions, practice systems, and more.

Here’s what you can discover in the learning zone for SAP Sapphire:

  • Explore curated learning and certification resources mapped to this year’s SAP Sapphire tracks for you to enhance your skills and prepare for SAP Certification exams. Unlock the learning zone now!
  • Save 25% on your SAP Certification, two attempts booking until June 12, 2026, and take the opportunity to get certified with SAP’s new practical, performance-based certification exams.
  • Benefit from complementary access to the free SAP Learning Hub, event edition until August 10, 2026. Deepen your learning with
    • expert-led live sessions
    • hands-on practice systems for a limited number of hours
    • access to the full SAP Learning content library

Curious to learn more? Dive into the learning zone for SAP Sapphire now. And make sure to register for SAP Sapphire to benefit from the certification discount and free access to the SAP Learning Hub, event edition.

entry.title: Zertifiziert für die Zukunft: Cpro Industry über SAP BTP, Cloud und Kundenvertrauen
entry.content:
Als SAP Gold Partner und zertifizierter Operations-Partner für SAP S/4HANA und SAP BTP unterstützt die Cpro Industry Projects & Solutions GmbH Unternehmen ganzheitlich – von der Einführung über die Weiterentwicklung bis hin zum Betrieb ihrer SAP-Landschaften. Mit den aktuellen Zertifizierungen unterstreicht das Unternehmen seine ausgewiesene Kompetenz und stärkt zugleich seine Position in zentralen Wachstumsfeldern der SAP-Strategie. Im Interview gibt Cpro Industry Einblicke in die wachsende Rolle von SAP BTP, in ihren Ansatz bei Cloud-Transformationen, in Erfahrungen aus dem Auditprozess sowie in die Bedeutung von Zertifizierungen für die Kundenkommunikation.

Können Sie uns Ihr Unternehmen kurz vorstellen und uns einen kurzen Einblick geben, welche Services Sie Ihren Kunden anbieten?

Die Cpro Industry Projects & Solutions GmbH, kurz Cpro Industry, begleitet Unternehmen bei der Einführung, Weiterentwicklung und dem Betrieb von SAP Lösungen. Als SAP Gold Partner und Teil einer breit aufgestellten Gruppe mit mehreren hundert SAP-Beraterinnen und -Beratern verbinden wir strategische Beratung, Projektumsetzung und langfristigen Application-Management-Service aus einer Hand. Unser Leistungsspektrum reicht von SAP S/4HANA in der Public- und Private-Cloud über SAP BTP und SAP CX bis hin zu branchenspezifischen Themen in Logistik, Supply Chain und Prozessdigitalisierung. Für unsere Kunden ist es vor allem wichtig, dass wir nicht nur die Technologie beherrschen, sondern auch die operativen Abläufe, Anforderungen und Realitäten im Tagesgeschäft verstehen.

2023 haben Sie sich erstmals dazu entschlossen, Ihr Serviceangebot von SAP zertifizieren zu lassen. Was hat Sie damals zu diesem Schritt bewegt, und wie beurteilen Sie diese Entscheidung rückblickend?

Die Entscheidung für die erste Zertifizierung im Jahr 2023 war für uns der logische nächste Schritt. Zum einen wollten wir unseren Status sichern und uns frühzeitig auf die neuen Zertifizierungsmodelle von SAP einstellen. Zum anderen war es uns wichtig, unsere Services weiterzuentwickeln und die Qualität für unsere Kunden noch konsequenter zu schärfen. Eine unabhängige Bestätigung durch SAP hat dabei einen hohen Stellenwert, weil sie unsere Arbeitsweise, Prozesse und fachliche Tiefe auch von außen nachvollziehbar macht. Rückblickend war diese Entscheidung absolut richtig. Die Zertifizierung hat uns nicht nur in unserer Positionierung gestärkt, sondern intern auch dabei geholfen, Standards, Verantwortlichkeiten und Qualitätsansprüche noch klarer zu definieren.

Ende letzten Jahres folgte eine erneute Zertifizierung, diesmal sowohl für SAP S/4HANA operations als auch für SAP BTP operations. Was waren die ausschlaggebenden Gründe, diesen Weg weiterzugehen und Ihr Portfolio erneut zertifizieren zu lassen?

Für uns war dieser Schritt folgerichtig. SAP BTP wird in den kommenden Jahren noch stärker zu einer zentralen Plattform für Erweiterungen, Integration und Innovation in modernen SAP Landschaften. Deshalb war es unser Anspruch, uns auch in diesem Bereich optimal aufzustellen und unsere Kompetenz nachvollziehbar zu belegen. Gleichzeitig begleiten wir seit vielen Jahren Kunden auf dem Weg zu SAP S/4HANA, sowohl in klassischen Transformationsprojekten als auch im Umfeld von RISE und der Private Cloud Edition. Der dazugehörige Betrieb und der anschließende AMS Service sind seit langem ein fester Bestandteil unseres Geschäfts. Vor diesem Hintergrund war es naheliegend, auch diese Leistungen erneut und gezielt zertifizieren zu lassen.

 

Welche Erfahrungen haben Sie in den vergangenen Jahren mit SAP BTP gesammelt, und welchen Einfluss hatten die Zertifizierung und das Audit auf Ihre Weiterentwicklung in diesem Bereich?

Wir haben in den vergangenen Jahren sehr deutlich gesehen, wie stark die Relevanz der SAP BTP zunimmt. In immer mehr Kundenprojekten spielt sie eine wichtige Rolle, etwa bei Integrationsszenarien, bei Erweiterungen nah am SAP Standard oder wenn bestehende SAP Anwendungen zukunftsfähig weiterentwickelt werden sollen. Genau deshalb wird die SAP BTP für uns auch in den nächsten Jahren ein klarer Fokusbereich bleiben. Die Zertifizierung und das Audit hatten für uns nicht nur einen formalen Wert. Sie haben uns geholfen, unsere Strukturen, Methoden und Betriebsprozesse in diesem Bereich weiter zu schärfen. Gleichzeitig unterstützen sie uns dabei, unsere Marktposition als etablierter und verlässlicher Partner für SAP BTP weiter auszubauen.

SAP richtet seine Strategie konsequent auf die Cloud aus und ermutigt Partner, ihre Kunden aktiv bei der Transformation zu begleiten. Wie unterstützen Sie Ihre Kunden derzeit bei ihrer Cloud Migration, und planen Sie, Ihr Leistungsportfolio künftig noch stärker auf Cloud Services auszurichten?

Wir unterstützen unsere Kunden bei der Cloud Transformation immer entlang ihrer konkreten Ausgangslage. Manche Unternehmen kommen aus einem gewachsenen ECC-Umfeld, andere prüfen einen Greenfield-Ansatz, wieder andere gehen mit RISE in die Private Cloud Edition oder bewerten den Weg in die Public Cloud. Unsere Aufgabe ist es, gemeinsam mit dem Kunden ein realistisches Zielbild zu entwickeln, die Transformation fachlich und technisch sauber aufzusetzen und anschließend einen stabilen Betrieb sicherzustellen. Genau darin liegt eine unserer Stärken, weil wir Strategie, Implementierung und Operations miteinander verbinden. Ja, wir werden unser Leistungsportfolio in den kommenden Jahren noch stärker auf Cloud Services ausrichten. Die Cloud ist für SAP und viele unserer Kunden längst kein Randthema mehr, sondern der zentrale Rahmen für künftige Weiterentwicklung, Skalierbarkeit und Innovation.

Welche Rolle spielen die SAP Zertifizierungen für Ihre Marketingaktivitäten und für die Kommunikation mit Ihren bestehenden und potenziellen Kunden?

Die SAP Zertifizierungen spielen für unsere Marktkommunikation eine wichtige Rolle, weil sie Vertrauen schaffen. In einem Umfeld, in dem viele Anbieter mit ähnlichen Leistungsversprechen auftreten, ist eine unabhängige Bestätigung durch SAP ein starkes Signal. Für Bestandskunden sind die Zertifizierungen ein zusätzlicher Beleg dafür, dass sie mit einem Partner zusammenarbeiten, der seine Leistungen strukturiert, nachvollziehbar und auf hohem Qualitätsniveau erbringt. Für potenzielle Kunden sind sie ein relevanter Vertrauensfaktor in der frühen Auswahlphase, in Ausschreibungen sowie in persönlichen Gesprächen. Auch das zugrunde liegende Competency Framework ist für uns wertvoll, weil es sichtbar macht, in welchen Bereichen unsere Kernkompetenzen liegen und wie konsequent wir unsere Services weiterentwickeln.

 

 

Informationen zum Zertifizierungsprogramm für Operations Partner und eine Übersicht über alle zertifizierten Partner finden Sie auf unserem SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Erfahren Sie mehr auf PartnerEdge.com

entry.title: How to Prepare for Your System-Based SAP BW/4HANA Certification
entry.content:
SAP BW, especially in its SAP-HANA-optimized version, SAP BW∕4HANA, is still the most widely used data warehousing solution with SAP. Consultants for SAP BW/4HANA are continuously highly requested by many enterprises. Whether you want to help within such SAP BW/4HANA projects, want to demonstrate your knowledge within your own organization, or apply for a new position – by proving your skills in this area, you can increase your chances.

 

So how do you prove your expertise? 

Understanding the certification format

You may have read or heard that SAP has re-designed the “SAP Certified - Data Engineer - SAP BW/4HANA” and the “SAP Certified - SAP BW/4HANA Delta“ certification. You now need to perform system-based tasks in a real system to verify that you possess the core skills required in the role of a Data Engineer in the SAP BW/4HANA area. It’s not just about memorizing concepts but actually applying them in a system. Check out this page with FAQ and an onboarding video that explains it:

https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-reporting-modelling-and-data-acquisition-with-sap-bw-4hana/exam?onboarding=true

You won’t be asked to simply define terms, but you’ll need to do the work, within a time limit of 180 minutes. This means, your preparation must be heavily practice oriented. Unlike theoretical exams, our hands-on certifications test your ability to navigate the SAP environment, build data models, and demonstrate real-world competency.

You should especially be able to demonstrate the following skills:

  • Creating sources and provisioning data from them,
  • Creating physical and virtual SAP BW/4HANA models,
  • Creating a data flow and filling data targets,
  • Applying concepts like data transformation, including data lookup, unit conversion, and currency translation,
  • Creating queries in SAP BW/4HANA,
  • Implementing authorizations in SAP BW/4HANA.

On the other hand, like in a real project situation, it’s now an open book exam: You are allowed to use any material or online resource to guide you, for example to find a transaction code or table name.

But don’t rely on looking up concepts or practical procedures: There is not much time to complete the certification tasks.

 

How Should You Prepare?

1. Review the theory:

Before diving into system-based preparation, it’s essential to understand what you’re preparing for.

Even though the exam is practical, you still need to understand the underlying concepts. Make sure you’re comfortable with:

  • Standard SAP BW architecture and exceptional options,
  • Data modeling layers (Staging, Harmonization, Reporting),
  • The purpose of specific objects and object types (e.g., InfoObjects vs ADSOs vs CompositeProviders),
  • Data flow processes (Extraction → Transformation → Loading)

 

2. Perform the course exercises:

Even though the exam tasks are different from the course exercises (which means, copying existing solution objects will not work for you), they are inspired by them. Therefore, invest most of your preparation time into repeating the exercises of our instructor-led trainings BW405, BW410, BW430, BW450, and BW465 – or demonstrations within the corresponding online material. Check the following blog for a list of the required courses:

https://community.sap.com/t5/data-and-analytics-learning-group-blog-posts/how-to-learn-sap-bw-4hana-and-get-certified/ba-p/14307472

 

Make sure that you know where you find the settings needed to fulfil the requirements of these exercises.

 

If you want to practice outside the setting of a classroom training, book an SAP Practice System similar to the ones that are used in the instructor-led class. Click here:

https://learning.sap.com/practice-systems?page=1

 

3. Practice your own example scenarios:

Some task steps of the system-based certification depend on earlier steps of the same certification. Therefore, try to think about a larger scenario. For example:

  • Create a DataSource in a source system
  • Replicate it to SAP BW.
  • Load data from the source.
  • Transform it.
  • Store it in an ADSO.

 

4. Timing and Quality management:

Hands-on exams are time-bound. It’s not enough to know how to do something—you must do it efficiently.

  • Practice tasks with a timer.
  • Identify steps where you slow down and intensify your training here.
  • Learn shortcuts and navigation tricks. For example, try to start a new exercise while a loading mechanism takes time without forgetting to return to the result for final steps.
  • Simulate exam conditions as much as possible.
  • Let someone else give you tasks and try to fulfil them as good as you can, even if something might be unclear to you.

In the system-based assessment, you will not be notified if you made an error. At the end, you submit the entire work, and then, there is no chance to correct it. So, focus on ways to test yourself if the objects you created really work as desired.

The technical object names are a crucial part of our verification mechanism. Train to be careful when creating the object names with the correct name. Pay attention to “_” (underscore).

Create a checklist of your common errors and review it before the exam.

 

5. Live Sessions

In addition to courses and practice systems, the SAP Learning portal offers live sessions that are useful for learning guidelines. Use especially the “Get Certified with BW/4HANA” series, starting on May 5th to ask questions. Click here to book it:

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/get-certified-sap-certified-data-engineer-sap-bw-4hana  

These sessions are delivered by subject-matter-experts (SMEs) from SAP Training

Just click below to explore our live sessions:

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions 

 

 

Taking the Certification Exam

When you feel prepared enough, it's time to take the certification exam.

Just click below for information and booking the certifications:

https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-specialist-sap-bw-4hana-delta

https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-reporting-modelling-and-data-acquisition-with-sap-bw-4hana

Book a time (see the image) when you will not be disturbed. 

 

Make sure that you take the exam in a place where you won’t be interrupted and with good internet. (If the internet connection is lost, you can log in again without losing your objects, but you’ll lose time.)

Make sure that you use the correct version of the tools as indicated in the task description.

As stated avove, make sure that you create objects exactly in the way it is demanded - especially regarding the technical names.

If something goes wrong:

  • Don’t panic
  • Check previous steps systematically
  • Fix the issue and move on

Remember: the exam is testing your practical problem-solving ability—not perfection 70% is enough.

Make sure that you really submit your work before the time is up.

 

Community

We are looking forward to getting your feedback on our certification and on our resources. Please feel free to use this community and connect with us and other learners and to ask your questions. Also let us know about topics you would like to have covered in future live sessions, so that our content fits your needs even better.

Good luck with your certification journey!

Alexander Scivos

entry.title: 📣 Big News for SAP HANA Pros - The Practical Certification Era is Here 📣
entry.content:
SAP is moving away from traditional multiple-choice questions toward performance-based, system-based practical exams. If you are looking to validate your skills as a Database Administrator, it’s no longer just about what you know, it’s about what you can do in a live environment.

The SAP Certified - Database Administrator - SAP HANA exam is now available in this new format. This means you'll be tasked with performing real-world administrative duties like deploying databases, configuration, backup, and setting up HA scenarios in an SAP HANA Cloud system.

🛠️ How to Upskill & Get Certified
Here are the essential resources to get you started:
▪️Prepare with the Learning Journey: Becoming a Certified Database Administrator - SAP HANA
▪️Take the Exam: SAP Certified - Database Administrator - SAP HANA

💡What is "SAP Certification Reimagined"?
SAP is transforming its entire certification portfolio to reflect the modern AI era. The Certification Reimagined initiative introduces:
▪️System-Based Exams: Hands-on tasks in a live, time-boxed SAP environment.
▪️Scenario-Based Assessments: AI-driven roleplays to test your consulting and architectural advice.
▪️Open-Book Format: You can use SAP Joule, the SAP Help Portal and SAP Learning during the exam - just like you would on the job.

🔗Learn more about the transformation here: Ready to get certified with SAP?

entry.title: Unlock the Power of Artificial Intelligence with SAP Learning: Courses, Certifications & More
entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming the way businesses operate, making it crucial to stay up-to-date with the latest advancements and best practices. SAP Learning is at the forefront of this revolution, offering a comprehensive suite of training opportunities tailored to boost your expertise and career in AI.

Featured Learning on SAP: Joule

Meet Joule—SAP’s intelligent AI assistant designed to accelerate business transformation. Whether you’re new to Joule or aiming to integrate AI-driven agents throughout your organizational system landscape, SAP’s curated courses will help you harness its full potential:

  • Introducing Joule: Get started with the basics and discover how Joule is reshaping intelligent business processes.
  • Boosting AI-driven Business Transformation with Joule Agents: Learn to deploy AI agents that unlock new efficiencies and innovation.
  • Implementing Joule Across Your Organizational System Landscape: Dive into a guided journey for seamless integration across your enterprise.

AI Foundation: Build, Solve, and Certify

Ready to put generative AI to work? SAP offers hands-on training and globally recognized certifications:

  • Solve Your Business Problems Using Prompts and LLMs in SAP Generative AI Hub: Leverage large language models (LLMs) and intelligent prompts to address real-world challenges.
  • SAP Certified – SAP Generative AI Developer: Validate your expertise and showcase your skill set with this in-demand certification, tailored for today’s data-driven environment.

Explore More: Expert-Led Sessions & Learning Journeys

SAP Learning goes beyond traditional courses. Benefit from expert-led live sessions and immersive, guided learning paths that ensure you not only gain knowledge but also practical, real-world skills. Our Artificial Intelligence Featured Learning Page is your one-stop shop to discover the full spectrum of AI offerings.

Why Choose SAP Learning for AI?

  • Skill-building for the future: Acquire knowledge that aligns with the latest business needs and technological breakthroughs.
  • Expert guidance: Learn from top professionals and AI practitioners.
  • Certification: Stand out with credentials that demonstrate your AI expertise to employers and peers.

Take the next step in your AI journey with SAP Learning.
Master artificial intelligence, get certified, and shape the future of business innovation. Visit the Artificial Intelligence Featured Learning Page to get started today!

entry.title: New Dates for Get Certified: SAP BTP Administrator Live Sessions
entry.content:
Since TechEd last year, the certification for SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Administrator is available. It is a system-based assessment, where you complete tasks in an authentic SAP Software environment – a real SAP BTP global account.

Currently, I am delivering the second time the Get Certified: SAP BTP Administrator live session track, which introduces you to this new format of hands-on assessments and summarizes the topics covered in the certification.

Because we are seeing a high demand for these live sessions, we just added additional dates for you in July, September, and November in 2026. Please join me in the journey to become an SAP BTP Administrator.

💡Hint:
The core of the Administrating SAP Business Technology Platform learning journey leading to the certification is the Operating SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP200) course. Check-out my blog post summarizing BTP200.

entry.title: 🚀 Exciting News: Updated E-learning courses on SAP Sales Cloud V2!
entry.content:
The world of sales is transforming faster than ever, powered by AI, intelligent automation, and the relentless pursuit of sales excellence. To help you stay ahead of the curve, we are thrilled to announce the updated release of our self-paced learning courses for SAP Sales Cloud Version 2, now available for free on learning.sap.com!

These are not just minor touch-ups — these updates bring fresh, relevant, and enriched content designed to meet you exactly where you are in your SAP Sales Cloud journey.

📚 The Courses Are Live — Start Learning Today!

We have updated two essential self-paced courses, each tailored to a distinct audience within the SAP Sales Cloud ecosystem:

🔹 For Business Users:
Exploring SAP Sales Cloud Version 2
Perfect for sales professionals, business users, and anyone looking to understand and navigate SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 with confidence. This course walks you through the platform from a practical, day-to-day usage perspective — empowering you to work smarter, faster, and more effectively.

🔹 For Administrators & Consultants:
Configuring SAP Sales Cloud Version 2
Built for system administrators, implementation consultants, and technical professionals, this course provides a comprehensive deep dive into configuring and managing SAP Sales Cloud Version 2. From system setup to advanced configuration, this is your go-to resource for mastering the platform from the inside out.

✨ What’s New in These Updated Courses?

We listened to your feedback, and we delivered. Here is what you can expect in the latest versions:

🤖 AI and Intelligent Agent Updates
Stay current with the latest advancements in Artificial Intelligence and AI Agents within SAP Sales Cloud. The updated content reflects the most recent product innovations, ensuring you are equipped with cutting-edge knowledge to leverage AI-driven capabilities in your daily sales and configuration work.

📦 Modularized and Improved Content
We have restructured and enhanced the course content for a smoother, more intuitive learning experience. Topics are better organized, easier to navigate, and more aligned with real-world application — so you spend less time searching and more time learning.

💬 Your Feedback, Incorporated
This update was shaped by you. Learner feedback has been carefully reviewed and integrated throughout both courses, making the content more relevant, accurate, and engaging than ever before.

🎬 New and Improved Videos and Demos
Experience richer, more dynamic learning with brand-new videos and refreshed demos that bring SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 features to life. Whether you are a visual learner or simply want to see the system in action, these enhancements make the learning experience more immersive and effective.

🎯 Who Should Take These Courses?

These courses are designed for anyone who works with or around SAP Sales Cloud Version 2:

  • Sales professionals and business users looking to maximize their productivity and get the most out of the platform
  • System administrators responsible for maintaining and optimizing SAP Sales Cloud environments
  • Implementation consultants and partners supporting SAP Sales Cloud deployments
  • IT professionals and decision-makers seeking a solid understanding of the platform’s capabilities

Best of all — both courses are completely free on learning.sap.com. There is no barrier between you and the skills you need.

🏁 Ready to Upskill? Start Now!

Whether you are new to SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 or looking to sharpen your expertise, these updated courses are your definitive learning path. Do not miss the opportunity to stay current, stay competitive, and unlock the full potential of SAP Sales Cloud.

👉 Start Exploring SAP Sales Cloud Version 2
👉 Start Configuring SAP Sales Cloud Version 2

Happy learning from the SAP CX Product Learning Team! 🎓

entry.title: IBM and SAP: Advancing AI Driven Operations in the Era of the Autonomous Enterprise
entry.content:
Fresh from SAP Sapphire 2026, one message is clear: enterprises are entering a new era where humans and AI agents work together to run business processes with unprecedented speed, precision, and autonomy. SAP’s vision of the Autonomous Enterprise – powered by Joule, AI agents, and a unified Business AI platform – signals a shift from isolated innovation to deeply embedded, operational AI across the enterprise.

Against this backdrop, the long-standing collaboration between SAP and IBM takes on even greater relevance. As organizations look to turn AI ambition into real operational outcomes, trusted partnerships and proven delivery models are critical. A recent milestone – the completion of IBM’s global SAP Business AI Operations audit – marks an important step forward in helping customers operationalize AI at scale.

 
The significance of this achievement goes beyond the milestone itself. The audit is a rigorous validation of delivery excellence, operational standards, and real-world AI capabilities – and IBM didn’t just meet the bar. It achieved a strong, comprehensive validation across all criteria, reflecting a high level of operational maturity and AI readiness. With this, IBM now holds all eight global SAP partner certifications, underscoring its end-to-end excellence and long-term commitment to SAP customers worldwide. For enterprises navigating cloud transformation and AI adoption, this sends a clear signal: IBM delivers trusted, future-ready SAP services at scale.

In this context, Emer Neville, Global Head of the Partner Excellence Center at SAP, and Joseph Msays, Vice President and Global Managing Partner at IBM, discuss how AI is moving from vision to value – reshaping day-to-day operations, accelerating time-to-value, and helping customers operationalize AI at scale.

Watch the full conversation from SAP and IBM leadership to see how AI is transforming enterprise operations in practice: Video

From Innovation to Operations: AI Becomes Business‑Critical

One theme emerged immediately: AI is no longer theoretical, but a defining force in enterprise operations. Joseph emphasized that organizations are already seeing AI reshape how they run their businesses today, delivering immediate, measurable value.

At the center of this shift is AI‑enabled operations. IBM is helping customers move toward conversational, ticketless support models that dramatically reduce resolution times and operational costs. Joseph also highlighted that the real breakthrough with AI is the convergence it enables. Generative knowledge, autonomous agents, automation, insights, and cloud technologies are finally coming together to drive tangible outcomes. As a result, customers no longer need to wait years to realize value; they can feel the impact almost immediately.

This transition to AI‑driven operations is closely tied to a strong SAP foundation. Customers are strengthening their SAP Cloud ERP environments by embracing RISE with SAP, SAP Joule, and clean‑core principles. Together, these elements provide the scalability, agility, and speed organizations need to modernize with confidence and embed AI securely across their core processes.

Where Customers Feel the Impact First

According to Joseph, customers adopting SAP Business AI typically experience the most immediate gains in operational efficiency. Faster issue resolution, reduced manual effort, and improved business prioritization deliver quick wins that build momentum. These efficiencies then pave the way for broader business transformation. Organizations begin applying AI to smarter forecasting, sharper financial insights, and better‑informed decision‑making. Increasingly, AI insights are being connected directly to decisions that drive revenue growth or reduce business risk.

As IBM’s alignment with SAP Business AI continues to deepen through ongoing certification, Joseph noted that customers feel even greater value from the partnership – both operationally and strategically.

Why Certification Makes a Difference from Day One

A key theme throughout the conversation was the role of ongoing certification. Joseph explained that when joint customers begin their SAP Business AI journey, they experience the difference immediately – not because of claims, but because of how IBM operates. From IBM’s perspective, certification is not just a checkbox exercise. Teams arrive already aligned with SAP Business AI best practices, proven delivery patterns, and scalable operating models designed to embed AI directly into day‑to‑day operations. This results in faster time to value, reduced risk, and AI solutions that work reliably at scale on a clean, cloud‑ready SAP foundation.

Just as importantly, IBM’s continuous certification and upskilling ensure customers are not only prepared for today’s AI capabilities but well positioned to evolve as SAP Business AI continues to advance – demonstrating the true strength and longevity of the SAP‑IBM partnership.

A Shared Look Ahead

As Emer concluded, AI – when combined with strong operational excellence and deep cloud transformation experience – is becoming a powerful accelerator for customers. Programs such as the SAP Operations Partner Certification reinforce best practices and give organizations confidence in their partner choice.

IBM’s achievements speak for themselves: a half‑century partnership with SAP, all eight global partner certifications, and a clear focus on turning AI into real operational and business value.

With SAP Business AI now taking center stage, both organizations are aligned around a shared goal: helping customers run better today, while continuously transforming for tomorrow.

As SAP Sapphire 2026 demonstrated, the future of enterprise technology lies in harmonizing AI, data, and business processes into integrated, autonomous systems. Innovations such as the SAP Business AI Platform and Autonomous Suite are redefining how work gets done – shifting from reactive operations to intelligent, self-optimizing workflows.

Together, SAP and IBM are helping organizations move beyond AI adoption to truly operate as autonomous enterprises.

 

Information on the certification program for operations partners, as well as an overview of all certified partners, can be found in the SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Visit OPC at SAP.com or on the SAP Partner Portal.

entry.title: How to get and stay Certified in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Asset Management ?
entry.content:
Here you can find the relevant details to get certified as an Implementation Consultant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Asset Management: SAP Certified - Implementation Consultant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud P

This certification verifies that you possess the core skills in the area of Asset Management required for the consultant profile and proves that you have an overall understanding and can implement the skills practically in projects to contribute to the success of the planning and implementation phases in a mentored role. The certification is relevant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA on-premise.

Please bear in mind:

  • The certification is valid for 12 months
  • You can extend your certification for another 12 months each time you successfully complete an assessment to stay certified
  • You’ll receive personalized notifications so you never miss your certification expiry date.

Here you can find the relevant details to stay certified as an Implementation Consultant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Asset Management: Stay Certified – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Asset Managem

We are happy to announce that Stay Certified – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Asset Management is now available based SAP S/4HANA 2025.

In order to stay certified you need an active SAP Learning Hub subscription to take the short assessment to stay certified.

entry.title: Stay Certified Process Clarification and advice for all current SAP FI and CO certifications
entry.content:
Are you ready to renew your certification validity through the Stay Current process for your Private or Public Cloud certification in Financial or Management accounting in SAP S/4HANA? Then, read on to understand how to prepare and ace your Stay Certified assessment!

There is a single place you can go to learn all you need to learn, our learning journey Applying Business AI Solutions and Expertise in SAP Finance:

 

After going through the learning journey, you can take the Stay Certified exam through the Manage Certification option in your profile on the learning.sap.com platform:

 

A quick reminder that the Stay Current assessments consist of 8 to 12 questions, are non-proctored and you can repeat the tests as many times as needed to pass. This means there is no risk in attempting to pass after going through the materials.

A couple further notes to save you from possible frustration: 

  • If you hold multiple certifications, you need to renew their validity by taking the (same) test for each of them. Sorry for this inconvenience as it is technically not possible to cross check the separate assessments currently.
  • There is a standalone assessment associated with the learning journey Applying Business AI Solutions and Expertise in SAP Finance. This is (unfortunately) independent to the stay certified process. So, though the standalone assessment that leads to the knowledge badge is "harder" there is again no impact on the stay certified process and you will need to access and pass the assessment in any case.

    Good Luck with your assessments!

entry.title: New SAP Certification – In Case You Missed It
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

 

in case this slipped a bit under the radar: a  NEW SAP CERTIFICATION  became available recently 😊

The SAP Certified – Integration Developer certification is a great opportunity to validate your skills and deepen your expertise in SAP Integration topics. 

 

👉  Learn more and explore the certification here:

https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-integration-developer

 

If you're looking to expand your knowledge or take the next step in your learning journey, this might be a great fit.

Curious – is this something you're considering?

 

Best,

Isabella

entry.title: Stay Certified in SAP Commerce Cloud with Confidence: Expert Guidance for Business Users and Develop
entry.content:
If you already hold an SAP Commerce Cloud certification, you know that staying at the forefront of e-commerce innovation is what sets you apart. To ensure your credentials remain as current as the technology itself, the SAP Certification program has transitioned to an annual validity model.

The "Stay Certified" Advantage - The Stay Certified program is specifically designed for individuals who are already certified. It offers several key benefits:

  • Keep Your Credentials Active: Instead of retaking a full associate or professional exam every year, you simply complete a shorter, focused assessment once a year.
  • Stay Ahead of the Curve: You gain immediate access to the latest product innovations, such as new AI capabilities, security enhancements, and platform updates.
  • Demonstrate Current Expertise: An active certification signals to employers and clients that your skills are up-to-date with the most recent SAP Commerce Cloud releases.

To support you in this transition, the SAP Commerce Cloud Learning and education team provides two new Stay Certified live sessions tailored for both Business Users and Developers.

Dual-Path Preparation: E-Learning + Live Expertise

To give you the best chance of success, we offer a blended approach to your preparation. You can start your journey with our dedicated e-learning courses:

  • Stay Certified E-Learning Course – SAP Commerce Cloud Business User
  • Stay Certified E-Learning Course – SAP Commerce Cloud Developer

These courses provide the foundational knowledge needed for the stay certified assessment. However, to easily master the new scope, we highly recommend joining our Live Sessions. Unlike self-paced learning, these sessions feature expert instructors who can provide real-time clarity, share deep-dive insights into new AI capabilities and security updates, and answer your specific questions on the spot.

1. Stay Certified: Business User – SAP Commerce Cloud

This session is tailored for professionals maintaining the C_C4H32 (SAP Certified - Business User - SAP Commerce Cloud) certification. We will guide you through the latest functional enhancements and strategic updates.

What to Expect:

  • Stay Certified Program Overview: Understand the transition to annual validity and the assessment requirements.
  • Latest Innovations: A topic-by-topic guide through new functional enhancements, including AI capabilities.
  • Platform Updates: Updates on infrastructure management and platform security from a business perspective.

Goal: By the end of this session, you will have a clear roadmap of the new exam scope and be ready to attempt the assessment to stay certified.

2. Stay Certified: Developer – SAP Commerce Cloud

Targeting the P_C4H34 (SAP Certified - Developer - SAP Commerce Cloud) certification? This session provides a technical deep dive into the latest platform innovations and developer-relevant updates.

What to Expect:

  • Technical Walkthrough: Insights into technical enhancements, performance improvements, and security updates.
  • Deep Dive into APIs & Integrations: Stay up to date with changes across APIs, cloud services, and integration patterns.
  • AI-Driven Capabilities: Learn about the latest developer tools and AI features within the SAP Commerce Cloud ecosystem.

Goal: This session serves as a practical guide to understanding the developer stay-certified exam scope, equipping you with the technical knowledge to pass your annual assessment.

Why Join These Live Sessions?

While these sessions are not a prerequisite for the assessments, they are an essential resource for staying current without the guesswork. By participating, you will:

  • Navigate the New Scope: Get expert guidance on exactly what has changed in the latest versions.
  • Save Preparation Time: Receive a structured guide to the most important "Stay Certified" materials.
  • Interact with Experts: Get clarification on new features directly from SAP trainers.

Are you ready to maintain your certified status?

Register today for the live sessions and stay ahead of the curve:

  • Register for Stay Certified Live Session: Business User – SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Register for Stay Certified Live Session: Developer – SAP Commerce Cloud

We look forward to seeing you there and helping you achieve your certification goals!

Happy learning!

entry.title: Stay Certified, Stay Ahead: New Assessment Questions for C_S4PM
entry.content:
Your SAP Certification is more than a milestone—it’s your proof of expertise. Today, we’re excited to share that a new version of Stay Certified assessment questions is now available for:

SAP Certified – Managing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Projects (C_S4PM)

Keep Your Skills Fresh and Relevant
Earning your certification is just the beginning. To maintain its value, it’s essential to stay aligned with the latest innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

The Stay Certified program helps you:

  • Stay up to date with product and process changes
  • Reinforce real-world project knowledge
  • Maintain a certification that reflects current expertise

What’s New in the Assessment?
The updated Stay Certified assessment includes:

  • Refreshed questions aligned with the latest releases
  • Real-life project scenarios for deeper understanding
  • A focused and efficient way to validate your knowledge

Check Your Deadline – Stay on Track!
To keep your certification valid, you must complete the Stay Certified assessment within 1 year after getting certified.
Follow these steps to check your due date:
learning.sap.com → My Learning → My Certifications → Manage Certification
You will also receive an email reminder 8 weeks before your due date—but why wait? Get ahead and complete it early!

Take Action Today
Staying certified is not just a requirement—it’s a commitment to excellence and continuous learning.
Take a few minutes today to check your status, explore the updated questions, and secure your certification for the future.

Let’s keep learning. Let’s keep growing. Let’s Stay Certified

entry.title: Learning and Certification for the AI Era: SAP Completes the Transformation of Certification
entry.content:
The Speed of Change Has Changed

In the age of AI, many would argue that we're experiencing something different from previous technology shifts: a collapse of time. New technologies, business models, and ways of working are emerging faster than organizations can absorb them.

According to LinkedIn's Talent Velocity research, 86% of organizations say they cannot upskill their workforce fast enough to keep pace with business demands.

As work changes, learning and certification must evolve as well. Professionals need more than theoretical knowledge, they need the ability to solve real-world challenges, work effectively with AI-powered tools, and continuously adapt as technology evolves.

Certification for the AI Era

The transformation of SAP Certification reflects this new reality.

Following the rollout announced earlier this year, the full SAP Certification portfolio now features practical, hands-on assessments designed to validate real-world skills rather than the ability to recall information. Learners can now choose from more than 100 certifications that assess their ability to solve business challenges, complete tasks in SAP systems, and apply their knowledge in realistic scenarios.

Just as AI is becoming part of everyday work, it is also becoming part of the certification experience. Through the open-book assessment approach, learners are encouraged to use available resources during their time-boxed exams, reflecting the realities of customer projects where professionals must work efficiently, solve problems under pressure, and leverage the tools available to them. This includes SAP Joule for Consultants, which is now available within selected certification exams to support learners in much the same way it supports consultants in their day-to-day work (phased rollout to follow).

More than 100,000 certification exams have already been taken under this new model, making SAP one of the first major enterprise software vendors to implement AI-enabled, performance-based certification assessments at scale.

As the transition to the practical exam portfolio has now been completed, the focus has shifted toward stabilization and continuous improvement. Learner feedback collected through post-exam surveys, support interactions, discussions here on SAP Community, and other channels directly influence roadmap priorities and enhancement planning.

Several improvements are already underway, including expanded language support and enhanced score reporting capabilities. Future score reports will provide greater transparency into performance across individual topic areas, helping learners better understand their results. If a learner does not pass an exam, these insights will make it easier to identify knowledge gaps and focus preparation efforts for the next attempt.

At the same time, SAP continues to invest in platform stability, user experience improvements, and support processes. The goal remains unchanged: creating a certification experience that is practical, transparent, reliable, and aligned with the realities of modern SAP work.

Learning the AI Way with SAP Learning Hub

Certification is just one side of the coin. SAP is also reimagining the learning experience.

To help SAP professionals upskill faster and in their preferred format, SAP Learning Hub now incorporates AI-assisted capabilities that make learning more personalized, interactive, and aligned with the way professionals learn today.

Learners can ask questions and receive answers grounded in SAP learning content in more than 80 languages. AI-generated study guides, quizzes, FAQs, podcasts, interactive AI-hosted learning experiences, and mind maps help learners engage with content in the formats that work best for them.

More than 13,000 early users have already embraced these capabilities to personalize their learning experience and accelerate skill development. Organizations are seeing measurable impact as well. LTIMindtree has reported significantly faster onboarding, while NTT DATA Business Solutions uses SAP Learning Hub as a key component of preparing talent for an increasingly AI-driven future.

The result is a more adaptive learning experience; one that helps learners spend less time navigating content and more time building practical skills.

A Milestone Year for SAP Skills

As SAP Certification approaches its 30th anniversary in 2026, the program is entering a new chapter.

Over the past three decades, SAP Certification has helped millions of learners validate their expertise and advance their careers. Today, the focus is evolving from knowledge validation alone to practical skill validation, helping ensure that learning, certification, and real-world job performance are more closely aligned than ever before.

Looking Ahead

The pace of change isn't slowing down, and neither is the evolution of SAP Learning and SAP Certification.

As AI becomes increasingly embedded in the way professionals learn, work, and solve business challenges, SAP will continue investing in experiences that help learners build practical, future-ready skills.

Join the Conversation

As we celebrate 30 years of SAP Certification, we're excited about what's next. Learning, certification, and work are becoming more connected than ever, and AI is helping make that possible. We look forward to continuing this journey together and hearing your feedback as we shape the future of SAP skills.

entry.title: Tuesday Upskilling: SAP CAP Certification
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

new week, new chance to learn something cool.

If SAP CAP and backend development on SAP BTP are topics on your radar, you might want to check out the SAP Certified – Backend Developer – SAP Cloud Application Programming Model certification.

It’s a great way to validate your skills and keep growing in cloud-native development.

👉 Have a look here:
https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-backend-developer-sap-cloud-programming-model

Would you consider adding this to your upskilling plan?

Best,
Isabella

entry.title: Start Your SAP Career on Coursera: Two Certificates to Get You There
entry.content:
Looking to start a career in SAP but not sure where to begin? You’re not alone and there’s now a clear, accessible path. Nearly 200 million learners are already using Coursera to start or change their careers, and SAP is part of that journey. 

Coursera offers two SAP Professional Certificates, designed for accessibility and real career momentum. Self-paced, available in over 25 languages, needing no prior SAP experience, and completable in as little as 12 weeks. 

They are built for early-career professionals, students, and anyone looking to transition into SAP-related roles, offering a structured way to build confidence before stepping into the ecosystem.   

Each Coursera certificate targets a distinct role in the SAP ecosystem: 

  • SAP Technology Consultant Professional Certificate: Builds the core consulting skills SAP customers and partners look for, from requirements gathering and stakeholder management to solution design and navigating full SAP implementation lifecycles using SAP Activate. 
  • SAP Business Analyst Professional Certificate: Focuses on business analysis fundamentals, process modelling with SAP Signavio, data analysis with SAP Analytics Cloud, and how to bridge the gap between business requirements and technical delivery. 

Both are available on Coursera and carry ACE® (American Council on Education) credit recommendations. This means learners may be able to apply their learning toward academic credit at participating U.S. and Latin American universities. 

 The pathway to SAP Certification 

 These certificates don’t stop at foundational knowledge; they open the door to what comes next.  From there, learners can deepen their skills on SAP Learning site through self-paced courses and expert-led learning journeys. 

Ready to take the first step? Whether you’re starting your own SAP journey or supporting the next generation of talent, these certificates offer a practical and proven entry point into the ecosystem.

entry.title: Two Decades of Proven Excellence: Syntax Achieves SAP Golden Certificate While Driving Innovation
entry.content:
How do you keep mission-critical SAP environments running flawlessly while continuously pushing innovation forward? In this interview, Syntax shares how its deep SAP heritage shapes a modern, global approach to operations – and marks a major milestone along the way. By achieving SAP certification for the 10th consecutive time, Syntax has earned the prestigious Golden Certificate, underlining its long-standing commitment to quality and operational excellence. Combined with newly achieved global certifications in SAP Security Operations and SAP Business AI Operations, this recognition highlights Syntax's ability to deliver secure, future-ready services at scale. From cloud transformation to the growing role of SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and AI, discover how Syntax helps customers turn SAP operations into a true driver of business value.

Could you start by telling us about your company and the services you offer?

Syntax is a global technology solutions and services provider with deep expertise in running and transforming mission-critical enterprise environments. We help customers manage complexity across cloud, applications, infrastructure, security, and end-user services, with SAP being one of our core strategic focus areas.

In the SAP space, we support customers across the full lifecycle: from advisory, migration, and implementation through to technical managed services, application management, optimization, and innovation. That includes operating highly business-critical SAP landscapes, supporting transformation to SAP S/4HANA and cloud-based environments, and helping customers unlock additional value through SAP BTP, automation, security, and AI.

What differentiates Syntax is our Boutique @ Scale approach. The ability to combine strong operational discipline with a forward-looking innovation mindset, tailoring every engagement to our customers' unique priorities. Customers come to us not only to keep their SAP environments stable and secure, but to evolve them as business needs change, backed by the agility and personal care of a boutique, delivered with the reach and resilience of a global enterprise.

 

 

Your SAP roots run deep, brought to Syntax through Freudenberg IT, whose SAP pedigree reaches back almost to SAP's founding. How has that heritage shaped who Syntax is today as an SAP operations partner – and is it still something you draw on?

Our heritage through Freudenberg IT has had a lasting impact on who we are as an SAP operations partner. It shaped a culture centered on reliability, accountability, process discipline, and long-term customer commitment, all of which remain essential when you are operating business-critical SAP environments.

That background gave us a very practical understanding of what enterprise customers need from an operations partner: not just technical expertise, but consistency, governance, responsiveness, and the ability to support transformation without compromising stability.

We absolutely still draw on that heritage today. While Syntax has evolved into a global organization with a broad portfolio and a modern cloud- and innovation-focused vision, the core DNA remains the same: trusted operations, deep SAP knowledge, and a commitment to helping customers run mission-critical systems with confidence.

 

Looking back at those early years of the partnership, what inspired your decision to seek your first service certification in 2002 – initially in Germany – and what then drove the decision to expand that certification globally in 2006?

From the beginning, we saw certification not simply as a badge, but as an important way to benchmark ourselves against SAP's own operational standards and to validate the quality of the services we were delivering to customers. Starting in Germany made sense because that was where our SAP operations heritage was strongest and where we had built deep experience in supporting complex customer landscapes. As customer expectations grew and our delivery model became increasingly international, expanding that certification globally was the next natural step. It reflected two priorities: first, giving customers the confidence that they would receive the same high-quality service wherever they operated; and second, creating a globally consistent operational framework across our teams. In that sense, certification supported both trust and scale.

Handover of the golden certificate, June 2026

 

How would you describe Syntax's SAP operations proposition to a customer who doesn't want to choose between operational reliability and continuous innovation?

Our message would be very simple: you should not have to choose between the two. At Syntax, we believe the strongest SAP operations model is one that protects the business today while enabling transformation for tomorrow. That means delivering stable, secure, and highly governed day-to-day operations while also helping customers adopt new capabilities in areas such as cloud, automation, SAP BTP, and AI.

We often describe this as helping customers optimize the "run" so they can invest more confidently in the "grow". In practice, that means reducing operational risk, increasing visibility, improving efficiency, and creating the right foundation for continuous innovation. For customers, the outcome is not just better uptime; it is a more agile SAP environment that supports long-term business change.

 

 

How do you experience the audits in practice, and what role do they play for your organization?

We see the audits as rigorous, constructive, and valuable. They are an important discipline for the organization because they require us to demonstrate - not just describe -  the maturity of our processes, controls, and service delivery practices.

In practice, audits help us maintain a strong level of operational consistency across teams and regions. They also create a useful feedback loop, highlighting where we are strong and where we can continue to improve. Internally, that reinforces accountability and continuous improvement; externally, it gives customers and SAP additional confidence in the quality and reliability of our services.

So for us, audits are not just compliance exercises. They are an important part of how we sustain operational excellence at scale.

 

For the first time, you achieved global certification for SAP security operations and SAP Business AI operations - can you tell us how your competencies around Security and Business AI operations are adding value to your service offerings?

These certifications are important because they reflect the evolving customer expectations. Today, customers want innovation, but they also want it delivered in a way that is secure, governed, and operationally robust. On the security side, our capabilities help customers strengthen the protection of their SAP landscapes through a combination of foundational controls, continuous monitoring, vulnerability management, privileged access governance, and broader security operations support. That adds value by reducing risk, improving visibility, and helping customers operate with greater confidence in increasingly complex hybrid environments.

On the Business AI side, the value is about helping customers move from interest to operational reality. It is one thing to explore AI use cases; it is another to run them reliably in enterprise environments. Our role is to help customers embed AI into their SAP landscape in a scalable, supportable way that is aligned with operational best practices. Together, Security and Business AI operations allow us to help customers innovate more safely and more effectively.

 

As SAP is strongly focusing on the cloud and encourages partners to support customers in their migration journey, how are you already helping customers in this area - and looking three to five years ahead, what role do SAP BTP and AI play in Syntax's vision and how do you expect your capabilities to evolve?

We are already supporting customers across the full cloud migration journey: from assessment and planning through migration, conversion, upgrade, and post-go-live operations. That includes helping customers modernize existing SAP environments, move to cloud-based operating models, and build the governance and support structures required for long-term success.

A major part of our value is that we do not view migration as a one-time project. We see it as part of a broader transformation of our operating model. Customers need a partner that can help them move, stabilize, optimize, and then continue to innovate.

Looking three to five years ahead, we see SAP BTP playing a central role as the platform for clean-core extensions, integration, automation, and data-driven innovation. We also expect AI to become much more deeply embedded - both in business processes and in the way SAP environments are operated and optimized. For Syntax, that means continuing to evolve our capabilities in platform operations, cloud governance, automation, observability, security, and AI-enabled service delivery. Our vision is to help customers build SAP operating models that are not only cloud-ready, but also innovation-ready.

 

If you could give one piece of advice to a customer who is just beginning to think about their SAP operations strategy for the next decade, what would it be?

If we had to highlight one key recommendation, it would be this: treat SAP operations as a strategic business capability rather than just a technical support function.

Over the next decade, organizations will need to balance resilience, security, cost efficiency, and continuous innovation far more closely than before. Those that succeed will be the ones who design their operating models early on, with standardization, automation, cloud readiness, and strong governance built in from the outset.

Ultimately, it is important not to separate your operations strategy from your transformation strategy. Reliable and well-governed operations are not just the foundation for stability - they are what make sustainable innovation possible.

 

Information on the certification program for operations partners, as well as an overview of all certified partners, can be found in the SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Visit OPC at SAP.com or on the SAP Partner Portal.

entry.title: Start a Career in SAP Consulting: A Structured Path on Coursera
entry.content:
The SAP Technology Consultant role sits at the heart of most implementations, translating business requirements into solutions and guiding projects from design through delivery. It’s also one of the most in-demand roles in the SAP ecosystem. 

For those looking to move into this space, the SAP Technology Consultant Professional Certificate on Coursera offers a clear and accessible starting point, no prior SAP experience needed. 

What the Coursera certificate covers 

The curriculum goes beyond product knowledge. Across seven courses, learners cover consulting skills, project management and tools like SAP S/4HANA A full hands-on implementation project puts it all into practice, producing real deliverables learners can mention in interviews. The final course focuses on career development and preparation for SAP Certification. 

Each course builds on the previous one. By the capstone, learners have worked through the full SAP implementation lifecycle twice: once with guidance, once independently. The certificate also carries ACE (American Council on Education) credit recommendations, meaning learners may be able to apply their learning toward academic credit at participating U.S. and Latin American universities. 

Who this is for 

The Coursera certificate is a strong starting point for anyone looking to enter SAP consulting. It's also useful for people already in the ecosystem who work alongside consultants and want a clearer picture of the full project lifecycle. 

From Coursera certificate to SAP Certification 

Completing the Coursera certificate is just the beginning. It builds foundational knowledge, with learners then deepening skills through SAP Learning site via self-paced courses and expert-led journeys. 

 Start learning on Coursera now: SAP Technology Consultant Professional Certificate
